name: Round Ligament Pain
advice: Normal stretching of the uterus. Move slowly, try pelvic tilts, and rest on your side.

name: Preeclampsia Warning
advice: URGENT: This can be a sign of preeclampsia, a serious condition. Contact your healthcare provider or go to the emergency room immediately.

name: Morning Sickness / Nausea
advice: Very common, especially in the first trimester. Eat small, frequent meals, try ginger or vitamin B6. If you cannot keep any fluids down for 24 hours, contact your doctor as this may be Hyperemesis Gravidarum.

name: Fatigue
advice: Normal due to hormonal changes. Rest when you can, eat a balanced diet, and listen to your body.

name: Braxton Hicks Contractions
advice: These are 'practice' contractions and are usually irregular and painless. Drink water and change positions. If they become regular, painful, or occur more than 4-6 times an hour before 37 weeks, contact your doctor.

name: Preterm Labor Signs
advice: URGENT: These are signs of preterm labor. Contact your healthcare provider or head to labor and delivery immediately.

name: Decreased Fetal Movement
advice: URGENT: A noticeable decrease in fetal movement requires immediate medical evaluation. Go to labor and delivery or contact your doctor right away.

name: Heartburn & Acid Reflux
advice: Common in pregnancy due to hormones and physical pressure. Avoid spicy/greasy foods, eat smaller meals, and don't lie down right after eating. Consult your doctor for safe antacids.

name: Mild Edema (Swelling)
advice: Mild swelling in the feet and ankles is normal. Elevate your feet, wear comfortable shoes, and stay hydrated. If swelling is sudden, severe, or in your hands/face, it could be a sign of preeclampsia (contact doctor).

name: Deep Vein Thrombosis (Blood Clot)
advice: URGENT: Pain, redness, and swelling in one leg (often the calf) can be a sign of a blood clot. Seek emergency medical care immediately.

name: Vaginal Bleeding (Severe)
advice: URGENT: Heavy or bright red vaginal bleeding is an emergency. Contact your doctor or go to the emergency room immediately.

name: Light Spotting
advice: Light spotting can be common, especially early in pregnancy, but should always be evaluated by a healthcare professional to rule out complications. Call your doctor.

name: Urinary Tract Infection
keywords: frequent peeing but little comes out
advice: May indicate a UTI, which needs treatment during pregnancy to prevent kidney infections. Contact your doctor for a urine test.

name: Frequent Urination
advice: Very normal as the uterus presses on the bladder. Keep drinking plenty of water, but if it's accompanied by burning or pain, call your doctor.

name: Ectopic Pregnancy Warning
advice: URGENT: Severe pain on one side early in pregnancy, sometimes with shoulder pain or dizziness, can indicate an ectopic pregnancy. Seek emergency care immediately.

name: Cholestasis of Pregnancy
advice: URGENT: Severe itching, particularly on the palms of hands and soles of feet without a rash, can indicate a liver condition called Cholestasis. Contact your doctor immediately for a blood test.

name: Lower Back Pain
advice: Normal due to shifting center of gravity and relaxing ligaments. Practice good posture, use supportive shoes, and try prenatal yoga or a heating pad (on low).

name: Sciatica
advice: Pressure on the sciatic nerve can cause shooting pain down the leg. Talk to your doctor or a physical therapist for safe stretches and relief options.

name: Constipation
advice: Hormones slow down digestion. Drink lots of water, eat high-fiber foods, and stay active. Consult your doctor before taking any laxatives.

name: Breast Tenderness
advice: Very normal, often one of the first signs of pregnancy. Wear a supportive, comfortable bra (like a sports bra or maternity bra).

name: Yeast Infection
advice: Yeast infections are common in pregnancy due to pH changes. Contact your doctor for a safe over-the-counter or prescription treatment.

name: Anemia / Iron Deficiency
advice: Dizziness and weakness can be signs of anemia (low iron) or low blood sugar. Sit or lie down immediately. Contact your doctor to have your iron levels checked.

name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
advice: Swelling in pregnancy can compress nerves in the wrist. Try wrist splints, especially at night, and mention it to your doctor at your next visit.

name: Mild Headaches
advice: Common due to hormones, stress, or dehydration. Drink water, rest, and try a cold compress. If the headache is severe and doesn't go away, it could be a sign of preeclampsia—contact your doctor.

name: Prenatal Depression / Anxiety
advice: Mental health struggles during pregnancy are common but require support. Please talk to your healthcare provider or a mental health professional for help and resources.
